story.description: A clip of nocturnal animals: nighttime pool party fun in bangalore
story.hook: Owls show why the silent feathers matters.
story.lead: Owls don't see the dark like we do. Their eyes are tubes, not balls, so they soak up every scrap of light. That's why they spot a mouse moving in a moonless Bangalore backyard. Here's what blew my mind: their retinas reflect light twice, like built-in night-vision goggles. Ever seen an owl's eyes glow. That’s not eerie, that’s science. So, why not just make their pupils huge.
